title: Product Traceability
objectives: Explain 'one step up / one step down' traceability

sections.markdown: **Product traceability** is the ability to follow a product's journey — from raw ingredients through manufacturing, distribution and retail to the consumer — and back again for recall. GS1 identifiers make each step referenceable with the same keys everyone agrees on.

sections.code:
'One up, one down' minimum:
  Every actor records who they received from (one up)
  and who they shipped to (one down).
  Combined across the chain -> full journey.

Five traceability questions (What/When/Where/Why/Who):
  What?  GTIN + batch/serial
  When?  Event timestamp
  Where? GLN / SGLN (read point, biz location)
  Why?   Business step (shipping, receiving, transforming)
  Who?   Party GLN

sections.markdown: The senior lens: traceability is **not just recording events** — it's ensuring the whole chain uses the **same identifiers** so events from Farm → Factory → Distributor → Retailer can be joined by GTIN + batch + SSCC without reconciliation. That's why GS1 keys are the bedrock.
Free-text 'source' fields ('supplier X, batch #12'). Recalls collapse: 'batch #12' means different things per supplier. Use GTIN + batch + GLN — machine-joinable and audit-friendly.

Model events with **EPCIS** (next topic). Recall SLA: from decision → target retrievals in < 4 hours for critical categories (pharma, high-risk food). Test recalls quarterly — you don't want to find out during a real one.

flashcards.back: Each actor records who they received from and who they shipped to — chained across the industry it yields full traceability.

flashcards.front: Five W's of a traceable event?
flashcards.back: What (GTIN/batch), When, Where (GLN), Why (bizStep), Who (party GLN).

flashcards.front: Why GS1 keys not free text?
flashcards.back: Only globally-agreed keys let separate actors' records join for cross-chain traceability.

quiz.q: The minimum bar for traceability across the industry is…
options: One up, one down at each actor
quiz.explain: Every actor tracking neighbors is enough to reconstruct chains.

options: Actors' records can be joined without reconciliation

quiz.q: Free-text sources fail at recall because…

challenges.text: Model a 'received / shipped' event with GTIN + batch + GLN.
Simulate a mock recall using an EPCIS-like event store.
Implement 'given a batch, list everywhere it's currently held' via aggregation events.
Design an industry-wide recall SLA + rehearsal process.

interviewQuestions: The five W's of a traceable event?
Why GS1 keys not free text?
How would you architect a recall in <4 hours?
What data must every event carry?
